title: preferences config
description: Shape of config/preferences.yaml: structured narrowing/ranking signals for offer search. Every field only NARROWS or RANKS within config/sources.yaml — it can never widen the boards/geos/languages allow-list. The subset-of-sources.yaml invariant is NOT expressed here (JSON Schema cannot reference another file); it is enforced by scripts/preferences/gmj_validate_preferences.py. additionalProperties:false is set at the root AND on every leaf object (salary/work_conditions/scope) so a misspelled top-level key (e.g. 'scopes:') is REJECTED rather than silently dropping the subset gate.

salary.description: Minimum compensation signal. Self-contained: additionalProperties:false is scoped to this object ONLY.

work_conditions.description: Work-arrangement signals. Self-contained: additionalProperties:false is scoped to this object ONLY.

scope.description: Scope-axis narrowing block. Each array MUST be a subset of the matching config/sources.yaml array — enforced at runtime by gmj_validate_preferences.py (the subset rule is not expressible in JSON Schema). Self-contained: additionalProperties:false is scoped to this object ONLY.

preferences.description: Ranked free-form soft preferences (first = highest priority).

ranking.description: Optional free-form numeric weights for ranking signals.

cover_letter_tone.description: Optional soft phrasing hint for cover-letter tone (e.g. 'warm', 'formal', 'concise, direct'). Influences ONLY phrasing/emphasis in the composed cover letter — it never licenses claims outside Gate A (truth) / Gate B (target-fit) and never widens the boards/geos/languages allow-list. Read by the hub and passed to the composer as a param; the composer never reads preferences.yaml directly.
